title: 05 — Refresh remaining direct dependencies and remove obsolete packages
objective: Bring all remaining direct dependencies to their latest stable compatible versions and ensure package.json accurately represents the versions the repository has validated.
userContext: Maintainers need dependency metadata that reflects tested direct package versions instead of relying on stale ranges or newer transitive lockfile resolutions, while avoiding unnecessary package additions.
currentBehaviour: After the runtime, workflow, framework, deployment, linting, TypeScript, and type-definition upgrades, remaining direct dependencies may still have outdated declared ranges, unused packages, or compatibility constraints that are not explicitly recorded.
requiredBehaviour: Every remaining direct production and development dependency must be inspected, upgraded where safely compatible with Features 01–04, removed if obsolete, or documented with a clear constraint and removal condition.
userExperience: Developers and maintainers can trust package.json and package-lock.json to represent the current tested dependency set, and dependency audit results are understood rather than ignored.
functionalRequirements: Inspect every remaining production and development dependency.
Compare declared version ranges with lockfile-resolved versions.
Upgrade each remaining direct dependency to the latest stable version compatible with the repository stack established by Features 01 through 04.
Explicitly assess gray-matter, remark, remark-html, zod, tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, postcss, and any other remaining direct package.
Review release notes for behavioural, security, compatibility, or deprecation implications.
Remove unused, redundant, deprecated, or superseded dependencies.
Avoid adding replacement dependencies unless necessary.
Update declared lower bounds so package.json reflects the versions actually tested.
Regenerate the lockfile using the repository-declared npm version.
Run dependency audit and investigate findings.
Record any dependency that cannot safely be upgraded, including the reason, latest tested compatible version, and condition that would allow the restriction to be removed.
technicalConsiderations: This feature depends on Features 01 through 04 so compatibility is evaluated against the established runtime, framework, deployment, linting, and type-checking stack.
Do not revisit framework-stack or toolchain-major upgrades that belong to earlier features except to account for already-merged outcomes.
Use the repository-declared npm version for lockfile generation.
A dependency should be removed when repository code no longer imports or needs it.
Security audit findings should be investigated for reachability, exploitability, fix availability, and compatibility impact.
Document intentional non-upgrades in a durable place that maintainers can revisit.
outOfScope: Changing the Node.js or npm baseline.
Major framework, deployment, ESLint, or TypeScript migrations already covered by Features 03 and 04.
Adding new packages for convenience when existing platform capabilities or repository dependencies are sufficient.
acceptanceCriteria: Every direct dependency and devDependency has been inspected against latest stable compatible versions.
gray-matter, remark, remark-html, zod, tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, postcss, and all other direct packages have explicit upgrade, removal, or constraint outcomes.
package.json declared ranges reflect versions actually validated by the implementation.
Unused, redundant, deprecated, or superseded direct dependencies are removed.
No replacement dependency is added without a documented necessity and compatibility justification.
package-lock.json is regenerated using the repository-declared npm version.
Dependency audit has been run and findings are investigated or documented.
Linting, type checking, tests, application build, Worker build, preview, and relevant validation tooling pass or have documented external-service limitations.
Any dependency that cannot safely be upgraded is recorded with reason, latest tested compatible version, and removal condition.
codexGuidance: Implement after DEV-004. Treat package.json and package-lock.json as the tested dependency contract, avoid expanding dependency surface area, and document any intentional compatibility hold.
